subject: [PATCH v6 3/3] fuse: add an implementation of open+getattr

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: Reviewer Bernd Schubert expressed concern that the proposed change to compound FUSE_OPEN and FUSE_GETATTR operations could introduce noticeable latency in fuse servers, specifically citing an sshfs example with high latency.
reviews.raw_body:
Personally I would see it as change of behavior if out of the sudden
open is followed by getattr. In my opinion fuse server needs to make a
decision that it wants that. Let's take my favorite sshfs example with a
1s latency - it be very noticeable if open would get slowed down by
factor 2.

Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Darrick Wong
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that NFS can corrupt O_APPEND writes unless synchronization is done at the application level, and suggested adding a check for time_before64(fi->i_time, get_jiffies_64()) to ensure accurate file size for append offset

Reviewer suggested that the fuse server could inform the client of the updated file size after every write operation, potentially by enlarging the write reply or adding a notification to keep the kernel up-to-date.
reviews.raw_body:
/me notes that NFS can corrupt O_APPEND writes if you're not careful to
synchronize writers at the application level...

---

I wonder, since O_APPEND writes supposedly reposition the file position
to i_size before every write, can we enlarge the write reply so that the
fuse server could tell the client what i_size is supposed to be after
every write?  Or perhaps add a notification so a network filesystem
could try to keep the kernel uptodate after another node appends to a
file?

Just my unqualified opinion ;)

--D

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch modifies the fuse implementation to compound FUSE_GETATTR with FUSE_OPEN only when necessary, specifically when file attributes are invalid or O_APPEND is set and the file's last access time has changed since it was opened.

reviews.author: Horst Birthelmer
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Horst Birthelmer expressed disagreement with adding an optional getattr to a write in a compound, citing concerns about supporting 'pages' for the compound and suggesting that notification could be handled with existing code

Reviewer questioned whether it's necessary to compound the getattr operation with open, suggesting that it should only be done when the attributes are actually invalid, and provided a possible implementation for handling O_APPEND
reviews.raw_body:
Bernd already had that idea, that we add an optional getattr to a write
in a compound.
If the fuse server supports it, you would get that data.
I'm really not happy about that idea, since we would have to support 'pages' 
then for the compound.

Regarding the notification. Isn't that doable with the current code already?

---

Thanks,
Horst

reviews.author: Miklos Szeredi
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that the current implementation of compound FUSE_OPEN and FUSE_GETATTR is still racy, and suggested using FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_INODE for perfect O_APPEND semantics; additionally, they proposed a delegation/lease mechanism in the fuse protocol to allow caching when the remote is not modifying
reviews.raw_body:
This can be done with FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_INODE.

Still racy.  If need to have perfect O_APPEND semantics,
FOPEN_DIRECT_IO is currently the only option.

It would be nice to have some sort of delegation/lease mechanism in
the fuse protocol to allow caching when remote is not modifying (which
is the common case usually) but force uncached I/O when there's
concurrent modification.

Thanks,
Miklos

reviews.author: Horst Birthelmer
reviews.summary:
Horst Birthelmer suggested adding a 'compound flag' to allow optional operations, which would enable sending open+getattr but only execute FUSE_OPEN if the fuse server doesn't support compounds, thus preserving current behavior and allowing for potential performance improvements.

Reviewer questioned the assumption that file attributes are locally cached, suggesting that getattr should only be compounded to open when attributes are actually invalid
reviews.raw_body:
OK, I will add a 'compound flag' for optional operations and don't
execute those, when the fuse server does not support compounds.

This way we can always send the open+getattr, but if the fuse server
does not process this as a compound (aka. it would be costly to do it), 
we only resend the FUSE_OPEN. Thus the current behavior does not change
and we can profit from fuse servers that actually have the possibility to
give us the attributes.

We can take a look at when to fetch the attributes in another context for
the other cases.

---

Thanks,
Horst

2026-03-03.patch_summary: This patch modifies the fuse implementation to compound FUSE_GETATTR with FUSE_OPEN only when necessary, specifically when file attributes are invalid or O_APPEND is set and the file's last access time has changed since it was opened.
